content.full_text:
CALIFORNIA LEGISLATURE
Senate

January 26, 1990

Ms. Colleen McGee
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Ms. McGee:

Each year when filing time arrives for the Statement of Economic Interests considerable confusion surrounds the effort.

This is particularly true in the various types of interest in Real Property.

The current Manual, at page 12, assumes a 50% interest in a land development company. This must be reported in Schedule A, "Investments Which Are Not Held Through A Business Entity Or Trust."

Yet in the third paragraph of the example "Schedule C-1 and C-2 - Interests in Real Property Held by Business Entities or Trusts," that same 50% interest must be reported. Obviously this contradicts the requirement of reporting in Schedule A for investments "Not Held Through A Business". The interest is either held through a business or not. It can't be both.

It seems to me that any time one owns a partial interest in real property or in a company there must be at least one additional partial interest. This necessarily means there is some form of business entity which has title to the property.

Over the years this has been puzzling. It has resulted in inconsistent reporting of real estate interests in and out of Schedule A. Last year I was advised by your agency staff not to include real estate partnerships and joint ventures in Schedule A anymore.

Ms. McGee  
January 26, 1990  
Page -2-

Please advise the correct schedule for reporting the following type of real property interests:

I have a 6% interest in a commercial building. I am one of a dozen owners through a formal limited partnership agreement. The partnership has a name. Should this be included in Schedule A, B, C-1, and C-2 in addition to Schedule D (Income)?

According to the manual example, this is similar to 50% ownership in the development company which must be reported under both A and C.

Very truly yours,

NICHOLAS C. PETRIS

NCP:tj
